---
title: "Summer Flounder Test"
author: "Sarah Gaichas"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(wham)
library(SPASAM.MSE)

main.dir = here::here()

```

# Make a summer flounder model

Use an old ASAP file from the rec_measures MSE to get started. This thing has 20 indices. What could go wrong?

Try reading the ASAP file into WHAM:

```{r, eval=FALSE}

mod.dir="sf-test"
write.dir <- here::here(sprintf("WHAMfits/%s/", mod.dir))
dir.create(write.dir)

sfasap <- wham::read_asap3_dat(here::here("assessments/F2021_2019_V1_MCMC.dat"))
inputsf <- wham::prepare_wham_input(sfasap)

mod <- fit_wham(inputsf, do.proj=FALSE,do.osa = TRUE,do.retro = TRUE,do.check = T,do.sdrep = TRUE)
saveRDS(mod,file=file.path(write.dir,paste0(mod.dir,".rds")))
plot_wham_output(mod, dir.main=file.path(write.dir),out.type="png")

```
That actually ran and produced output, gives the right trends

```{r}
knitr::include_graphics(here::here("WHAMfits/sf-test/plots_png/ref_points/FSPR_relative.png"))
```

Convergence? no

```{r}

mod <- readRDS(here::here("WHAMfits/sf-test/sf-test.rds"))
wham::check_convergence(mod)


```

We'll need to simplify this
